page: Microsoft Azure D48a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DAv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes dedicated allocation. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1229 GB of local storage. No GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance is also rated strong in stress-ng, though Geekbench single-core results fall into the middle 50%. Memory performance is a notable highlight, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10%, despite uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidths remaining in the middle 50%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, ray tracing, and image processing benchmarks also demonstrate top 25% performance.

While the server excels in multi-threaded tasks and memory-intensive operations, it exhibits limitations in specific workloads. For instance, LLM inference text generation using a large 70B model is weak, falling into the bottom 25%, whereas text generation with a small 135M model is strong. Database operations under PassMark and Redis SET operations both register average performance. This resource profile and performance distribution make the server suitable for multi-threaded applications, general-purpose web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale data processing workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D48a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores. Operating at 3.525 GHz, this x86_64 instance features 192.0 GB of RAM and 1229 GB of local storage, with no GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. The PassMark memory score is particularly high, placing in the top 10%. However, single-core metrics and large-block memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, and LLM inference for large models is weak. This configuration is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads requiring a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of memory per core.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48a_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 48 vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring forty-eight vCPUs, high-capacity local storage, and strong multi-core processing performance for diverse workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory and 1229 GB of local storage
Top 10% PassMark memory score and top 25% multi-core CPU performance

Suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads
